decks.notes: The main focus of this deck is to utilize the combo of Phyrexian Dreadnought and Stifle to quickly summon a powerful creature while preventing opponents from countering or disrupting the play. The deck also includes cards to manipulate the top of the library, draw additional cards, and counter spells to maintain control of the game. With a focus on speed and disruption, this combo-stiflenought deck aims to overwhelm opponents and secure victory through efficient and strategic play.

The deck focuses on utilizing aggressive creatures like Terravore along with land destruction cards like Armageddon to disrupt the opponent's resources and overwhelm them quickly. It also includes cards like Weathered Wayfarer and Sylvan Library to ensure consistent land drops and card advantage. The strategy is to quickly ramp up mana production, disrupt the opponent's mana base, and efficiently beat them down with large creatures. The deck aims to overpower the opponent before they can establish their own game plan.

The main focus of this Aggro Goblins deck is to overwhelm the opponent with a fast and aggressive creature strategy. By utilizing cards like Goblin Lackey and Goblin Matron to put multiple Goblins onto the battlefield quickly, combined with cards like Goblin Piledriver and Goblin Warchief to increase their power and efficiency, the deck aims to quickly deal damage to the opponent before they have a chance to establish their own game plan. Cards like Wasteland and Lightning Bolt provide additional disruption and removal options to help clear the way for the Goblins to attack.

The main strategy of this deck is to quickly fill the graveyard with enchantments using cards like Attunement and Intuition, then cast Replenish to bring them all back into play. The key combo involves using Opalescence to turn these enchantments into creatures, while also utilizing Parallax Wave to control the board and protect the player. Decree of Silence helps to counter opponent's spells and Mana Leak can also be used for additional disruption. The deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with a powerful board presence and lockdown their ability to effectively respond.

The main concept of this BW Control deck is to control the game by using a combination of removal spells (such as Swords to Plowshares and Vindicate), hand disruption (Duress and Cabal Therapy), and board wipes (Wrath of God and Decree of Justice). The deck also includes cards that provide card advantage and recursion (such as Skeletal Scrying and Eternal Dragon) to outvalue the opponent. The deck aims to disrupt the opponent's strategy while maintaining card advantage and eventually win the game through attrition.

The deck focuses on aggressive plays using efficient creatures like Savannah Lions and Patrol Hound, while also taking advantage of the Madness mechanic with cards like Wild Mongrel and Basking Rootwalla. The deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with fast creatures and pump spells like Rancor, backed up by removal in Swords to Plowshares and disruption with Wasteland. The inclusion of Gaea's Cradle provides additional mana ramp, allowing for explosive turns. Overall, the deck aims to maintain pressure and tempo while utilizing efficient creatures and combat tricks for a decisive victory.

The main focus of this Mono Black Aggro deck is to quickly overwhelm the opponent with a combination of efficient creatures like Withered Wretch, Ravenous Rats, and Hypnotic Specter, backed up by disruptive spells such as Cabal Therapy, Duress, and Smother. Additionally, the deck's use of resource denial with cards like Wasteland and Mishra's Factory can further hinder the opponent's game plan. The inclusion of card advantage engines like Phyrexian Arena helps to ensure that the deck can maintain pressure and control throughout the game.

The focus of this deck is to control the game by using counterspells, removal spells, and card draw to maintain card advantage. The key card in this archetype is Psychatog, which can grow in power quickly and become a win condition. The deck also includes board wipes like Armageddon to disrupt your opponent's strategy. The goal is to outvalue your opponent by efficiently managing resources and maintaining control of the game until you can close it out with a powerful finisher like Psychatog.

The main focus of this deck is to quickly ramp up mana with efficient Elf creatures and Gaea's Cradle in order to play powerful creatures and activate abilities. The deck includes cards like Wirewood Symbiote and Quirion Ranger to generate extra mana and reuse key creatures, while also running Survival of the Fittest for consistent creature selection. The deck aims to overwhelm opponents with a fast and aggressive strategy, utilizing cards like Deranged Hermit and Masticore to further pressure the opponent and maintain board control.

This deck aims to achieve dominance by controlling the field with a mix of creature removal, land destruction, and hand disruption. The goal is to outvalue the opponent by using efficient creatures like Krosan Tusker, Ravenous Baloth, and Treetop Village while keeping the opponent's threats in check with cards like Smother and Pernicious Deed. The deck also focuses on graveyard recursion with Genesis and disrupting the opponent's game plan with Duress and Cabal Therapy. Overall, The Rock aims to grind out a win through resource management and strategic decision-making.

This deck focuses on utilizing the "Stiflenought" combo to quickly assemble a powerful threat on the board. By combining Phyrexian Dreadnought with Stifle, the deck aims to cheat a massive creature into play for a low cost. The deck also includes card draw and disruption spells to control the game until the combo can be assembled. With a low land count and a focus on tempo plays, this deck looks to overwhelm opponents with a quick, game-ending combo.

The main objective of this deck is to control the game through counterspells and removal spells while also growing the power of Quirion Dryad and Psychatog. Utilizing card draw and manipulation, the deck aims to outvalue the opponent and maintain control of the board. The deck thrives on efficient spells and strong creatures to overwhelm the opponent and secure victory. By balancing defense and offense, the deck can adapt to various situations and emerge victorious in the end.

The main idea of the following deck is to utilize the combo of Tinker and Fling to quickly and efficiently disrupt the opponent's strategy. By using cards like Phyrexian Devourer, Altar of Dementia, and Oath of Druids, the deck aims to quickly assemble the pieces needed for the combo while also having ways to protect itself from disruption. With a focus on mana acceleration and tutors like Enlightened Tutor and Lim-Dûl's Vault, the deck is designed to consistently and effectively execute its game plan in a Premodern 1 vs 1 format.

This deck revolves around playing aggressive creatures like Patrol Hound, Savannah Lions, and Wild Mongrel while utilizing Madness cards like Basking Rootwalla and Arrogant Wurm. The key strategy involves quickly building up a board presence and applying pressure to the opponent, while also having access to powerful removal spells like Swords to Plowshares. The inclusion of cards like Tireless Tribe and Call of the Herd provide additional versatility and options for the player, allowing for different pathways to victory. Additionally, the deck includes utility lands like Mishra's Factory and Wasteland for added disruption.

This deck focuses on quickly assembling the Phyrexian Dreadnought combo by playing cards like Stifle and Counterspell to protect the combo. It also includes card draw spells like Gush and Accumulated Knowledge to help find the combo pieces faster. The deck aims to control the game through counterspells while simultaneously setting up for a powerful, game-winning combo. The key strategy is to efficiently find and protect the Phyrexian Dreadnought to overwhelm the opponent with a massive creature early in the game.

The deck focuses on assembling the combo of Hermit Druid and Phyrexian Devourer to quickly fill the graveyard, followed by using Krosan Reclamation to shuffle key creatures back into the library. This allows for recurring loops with cards like Karmic Guide and Squee, Goblin Nabob, ultimately leading to explosive plays with cards like Animate Dead and Triskelion. The deck aims to quickly set up an overwhelming board presence and win the game before the opponent has a chance to respond.
